Info callout focuses on what the developer must configure ('must register', 'See the code samples'), not abstract architecture. This is scope-limited since TASK-004 only added the info callout and code sample updates, not rewriting the whole page.

summary.narrativeSummary: All technical claims in the TASK-004 documentation updates are verified against source code. The four core changes — (1) AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, ApplicationRole> in three code samples, (2) AddRoleStore<ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>> in the password reset sample, (3) the new Prerequisites info callout about member role services, and (4) the member_roles_xp cross-reference — are all technically accurate. ApplicationRole is confirmed as a real role class extending IdentityRole<int> and mapping to MemberRoleInfo (not a no-op stub). ApplicationRoleStore<TRole> is a real IRoleStore<TRole> implementation with the correct generic constraint. The NoOp* classes are confirmed [Obsolete] with messages pointing to these exact replacements. All code examples use correct type names, generic parameters, and method signatures consistent with both the source code and sibling documentation pages. The 4 unverifiable claims are about ASP.NET Core Identity defaults and database table names — these are pre-existing content not modified by TASK-004.
